label: 238j Evaluation of programs

Amendments1993— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows: “Such portion as the Secretary may determine, but not more than 1 per centum, of any appropriation for grants, contracts, or other payments under any provision of this chapter, the Mental Health Systems Act, the Act of (Public Law 568, Eighty–third Congress), or the Act of (), for any fiscal year beginning after , shall be available for evaluation (directly, or by grants or contracts) of any program authorized by this chapter or any of such other Acts, and, in the case of allotments from any such appropriation, the amount available for allotment shall be reduced accordingly.”Pub. L. 103–183Public Law 85–151August 5, 1954August 16, 1957June 30, 19701981— struck out references to Mental Retardation Facilities Construction Act and Community Mental Health Centers Act.Pub. L. 97–351980— inserted reference to Mental Health Systems Act.Pub. L. 96–398

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Such portion as the Secretary shall determine, but not less than 0.2 percent nor more than 1 percent, of any amounts appropriated for programs authorized under this chapter shall be made available for the evaluation (directly, or by grants of contracts) of the implementation and effectiveness of such programs.

children.heading: Report on evaluations
children.label: b Report on evaluations
children.text: Not later than February 1 of each year, the Secretary shall prepare and submit to the Committee on Labor and Human Resources of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives a report summarizing the findings of the evaluations conducted under subsection (a).
